ops.title: Fleet-wide Models routing table (v2 + check-back + security lane, 2026-07-03): opus-4.8 xhigh = implementation default (delegate, don't edit inline on fable — main-loop nudge enforces), gpt-5.5 via codex = code/diff review + security review/audit + verif

ops.body:
On 2026-07-03 the Models routing table flipped (user: "escalating to opus when fable
fails makes no sense — opus is less smart and ~2x cheaper"). Source of truth:
`cc-skills/plugins/repo-bootstrap/skills/repo-bootstrap/templates/base/CLAUDE.md`
(§ Plan Execution & Orchestration), deployed byte-identical to all 31 fleet repos.

The policy: **opus-4.8 `xhigh` is the default for delegated work** — when in doubt, opus;
implementation subagents land here unless very sensitive or error-prone. **fable-5 keeps**
orchestration, review, hard planning/design/diagnosis, ALL prose/writing (never down-route
writing), sensitive/error-prone implementation, and is the only escalation target —
escalation flows opus→fable, never fable→opus (that's a down-route: opus is Int 8 / Cost 4
vs fable Int 9 / Cost 2). Context-window offload is not a routing cue — route by task type.
**gpt-5.5** gained a production lane: well-scoped edits to existing code (little net-new
code) via the codex skill; from workflows/subagents (model param takes only Claude models)
use a thin `model: sonnet, effort: low` wrapper that writes a self-contained codex prompt
and runs the codex skill. sonnet-5 recon (never haiku except single-fact mechanical),
gpt-5.5 also for second opinions/imagegen/rote work. **Effort**: `xhigh` everywhere; the
sole exception is fable implementation, which may run `high` ("speed outranks quality"
escape removed); `max` only after xhigh falls short; verification ≥ implementer tier.

**Why:** opus-4.8 is ~2x cheaper than fable AND less capable, so "escalate to opus after
fable misses" was backwards; user wants aggressive opus delegation to conserve fable spend
for judgment-heavy lanes.

**How to apply:** capt-hook ≥6.3.0 (general pack 0.8.0) `packs/general/models.py` enforces:
haiku gate + 'mechanical' escape, prose→fable `llm_gate` (still blocking, judge-confirmed:
the prefilter is a dependency-parsed NLP `Clause` — a writing verb must GOVERN a prose
artifact as its object, negated asks like "do NOT edit CHANGELOG.md" subtracted before any
LLM call; a small-model judge then checks the prose is the spawn's DELIVERABLE;
Explore/claude-code-guide skip outright), Explore→sonnet rewrite, workflow-haiku nudge,
workflow-prose `llm_nudge` (same clause prefilter via prose-gated `ProseSpawn` /
`WorkflowScriptSource` contexts; the workflow context quotes every model-pinning line plus
the matched prose sentences so the judge can tell a pinned code stage from an unpinned
prose stage), and the **fable-implementation `llm_nudge`** (fires only on clearly-routine
implementation staying on fable, uncertain → silent; spawn details via the required
`DelegatedSpawn` context). Clause scanning runs on tagger-de-noised text (paths dropped,
brackets/word-edge quotes blanked, extensions stripped, imperative writing verbs given a
determiner) — see `prose_deliverable_sentences`. Gotcha learned there: zero-arg `super()`
breaks inside `@dataclass(slots=True)` subclasses. Inline tests stub the LLM (fire=true by
default; `Input(llm={"fire": False})` overrides per-test since 6.2.0) but NLP runs real, so
negation/clause behavior is condition-testable. Fixed after four live false positives,
2026-07-03; probe 23/23, live dispatch eval 9/9 twice. Guard test:
`cc-skills/plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_scaffold.py::test_claude_md_routes_models_not_max_effort`
(asserts `when in doubt, opus`, `| opus-4.8 | 4 | 8 | 8 |`, negatives on the old phrases).
Never suggest reverting settings.json `effortLevel: "max"` — user explicitly kept it.

**v2 (2026-07-03 PM), after the zero-fire diagnosis.** The fable-impl nudge had fired ZERO
times: it watched `Agent|Task` only, while fable implemented via direct Edit/Write on the
main loop (232 edits vs 27 spawns over 40 sessions). Second flip (user: diff-review
fan-outs "should be using gpt 5.5"): **code/diff review (finder+refuter passes) and bug
diagnosis route to gpt-5.5 via codex**, escalation gpt-5.5→fable when the output misses;
fable keeps design/architecture review, approach judgment, prose review, and the
**synthesis/accept-reject pass** over findings. Implementation directive is now explicit:
"delegate it rather than editing inline on fable". **Every plan's Workflow Plan table names
model AND effort per phase** (e.g. `opus xhigh ×4`, `sonnet low → codex`) — the older
model-only clause had only ever reached 1/30 fleet repos. Enforcement (capt-hook 6.4.0,
general 0.9.0): main-loop impl `llm_nudge` on `Edit|Write|MultiEdit` (gates: `when=` not
`is_subagent` + ≥400 chars, `FilePath` code globs project-only, test-glob skip_if,
`max_fires=1`, ambient Before/AfterEdit carry the text, `InlineEdit` context names the
file), Agent/Task + WorkflowScript review/diagnosis nudges (regex prefilter
`review|refut|adversari|audit|correctness|diagnos`, judge keeps design/synthesis/prose
review silent; codex-wrapper stages pinned sonnet are correctly-routed → skip/false).
`WorkflowScriptSource` is now the generic pins+source context; prose prefilter lives in
`ProseWorkflowScript`. Live-smoked end-to-end 2026-07-03: impl nudge fires on synthetic
main-loop Write, review nudge fires on the exact "sweep the diff for go-correctness"
fan-out prompt, subagent edits silent. Framework gotchas: `File` export is the file object
— the path condition is `FilePath(*globs, project_only=True)`; `is_subagent` is a property
(not callable); test `Input` fields are `file=`/`content=`/`old=`. Deployed: 31-repo sweep
(Models block + Workflow Plan bullet normalized from 3 drift variants), repo-bootstrap
0.31.0 / codex 0.5.0 (codex skill now triggers on review/diagnosis asks), 3 previously
unwired repos (cc-skills, cc-sentiment, daily) wired with hooks + `.claude/hooks/packs.toml`.
**bioqa is deliberately NOT wired the standard way** — it's the capt-hook dev/dogfood repo:
its wiring lives in a TRACKED `.claude/settings.local.json` running
`uvx --refresh --from git+…captain-hook@main capt-hook run <Event>` (source @main, 6 events
incl SubagentStop) over a bespoke `.claude/hooks/` tree; adding standard settings.json
hooks + packs.toml DOUBLE-FIRES every event (Claude Code merges both settings files' hook
arrays) — I did exactly that on 2026-07-03 and reverted (bioqa main = the revert commit).
Check settings.local.json before calling a repo unwired.
bioqa/claude-pool/daily lack a Writing Plans AGENTS.md section (bullet skipped). Guard
test also asserts `rather than editing inline on fable`, `code/diff review`,
`bug diagnosis`, `synthesis/accept-reject`, and the partial's `model and effort` clause.
**Check back on the unexpected (2026-07-03, later same day).** New standing rule in the
Models block: a delegated agent hitting a task-shape surprise (scope change, invalidated
assumption, task not as described) STOPS and returns early with findings + 2-4 concrete
options for the fable orchestrator to pick — never improvises a detour, never punts the
decision to a cheaper model (a sonnet codex-wrapper doesn't ask codex what to do next).
Transient friction (retries, rate limits, external failures) stays autonomous per AGENTS.md
General Rules — the rule is scoped to shape-change so it can't be read as "stop on any
error". Claude subagents/workflow agents get it via CLAUDE.md inheritance; gpt-5.5 doesn't
see CLAUDE.md, so the codex SKILL.md carries it twice (wrapper "never absorbs a surprise" +
Step-3 premise-invalidated guidance). Guard test:
`test_scaffold.py::test_claude_md_check_back_on_the_unexpected` (asserts template + mirror +
codex skill copies). Runtime enforcement: **general-pack `detours` `llm_nudge`** (added capt-hook
6.5.0/pack 0.10.0, `packs/general/detours.py`; as of capt-hook 8.1.0 the pack is 0.12.0 and
also carries the delegated-prose nudge pair in `models.py`, [[delegated-prose-points-at-skill]]) — PostToolUse warn, cheap gate = detour
phrasing signals ("while I'm here", "might as well", "let me also"; threshold 2, window 8)
co-occurring with `Tool("Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit|Bash")`, judge compares current
action vs the user's actual request in the transcript; prerequisites/pre-authorized/
already-offering-options/uncertain all stay silent; warn text tells the agent to propose
2-4 options (or return early to its orchestrator if delegated). Hook count is baked in THREE
places when adding a general-pack module: `GENERAL_HOOKS` set + the `"N hooks"` CLI
assertion in `tests/test_packs.py` + the `pack list` sample in `docs/guide/packs.qmd`.
Deployed: repo-bootstrap 0.32.0 / codex 0.6.0, 33-repo fleet sweep (32 pushed + cc-context
covers the jj-only cc-context-anchors workspace, which shares cc-context's origin — don't
double-commit stale workspaces that share a remote).
**Security lane (2026-07-03, evening).** User: "security stuff should also be routed to
gpt-5.5". Scope decided via AskUserQuestion: **security review/audit and verification of
security-sensitive code (auth, input validation, crypto, secrets) → gpt-5.5**; security-
sensitive IMPLEMENTATION stays fable (carve-outs in the fable row, fable-impl nudge, and
inline-edit nudge untouched). Second fix folded in from a live incident: an agent hit the
review→gpt-5.5 nudge in a repo with the stale pre-v2 table ("advisory — never review") and
refused the routing — that table only lived in cc-context-anchors, which is now DELETED
from disk, but the v2 Effort line ("verification at same-or-higher tier") still read as
contradicting the gpt-5.5 lanes, so it now says table-routed gpt-5.5 lanes **count as
same-tier** at xhigh (escalation + synthesis stay fable; rule bites on un-routed choices).
Enforcement (capt-hook 8.2.0 / pack 0.13.0): both review/diagnosis nudges' prefilters are
`review|refut|adversari|audit|correctness|diagnos|root.?caus|secur|vuln|pentest` PLUS
`verif\w*` only within ~160 chars of a security noun (auth/crypt/secret/sanitiz/inject/
input.?valid/token/session) — bare `verif` would put the judge on every routine
verification spawn; prefilter-negative inline tests (plain-verify `Allow()` with NO `llm=`
stub) pin that property. Guard-test anchors (test_claude_md_routes_models_not_max_effort):
`security review/audit`, `verification of security-sensitive code`, `count as same-tier`,
`very sensitive or error-prone implementation`. Deployed: repo-bootstrap 0.34.0 / codex
0.7.0 (plugin.json description drift from 0.5.0 also fixed), fleet = two-line stamp
(gpt-5.5 row + Effort line) across all 31 repos; bioqa's Codex Verification bullet dropped
its stale `-m o3`. Still NO Models table: cc-sentiment, slop-cop (pending bespoke pass).
The AGENTS.md plateau bullet (second opinion on algorithmic/security-sensitive code) was
deliberately left unchanged. During the captain-hook edit a concurrent session hijacked the
shared working copy mid-task — the finished edits survived inside the prepared jj change
(auto-snapshot), landed via `jj describe -r <change>` + `git push origin <sha>:refs/heads/main`
(local main bookmark was conflicted — never wrestled it), and the CLAUDE.md stamp went in as
a git plumbing commit (hash-object/mktree/commit-tree) with zero working-copy contact.
**codex-wrapper agent (2026-07-08).** The "sonnet wrapper runs the codex skill" pattern is
retired: `Skill(codex)` from a schema-bound subagent (any workflow `agent(..., {schema})`
step) leaks the caller's `StructuredOutput` tool into the `context: fork` execution; a fork
ending its turn on it returns the bare "Skill execution completed" stub and the answer is
silently discarded (anchors-r2 2026-07-07: 15/18 calls, 100% correlation; forks also
self-reviewed as Claude instead of exec-ing codex — lane-fidelity break). codex plugin
0.9.0 ships `agents/codex-wrapper.md`: spawn `codex:codex-wrapper` (Agent/Task
`subagent_type`, workflow `agentType`) with the self-contained question as the prompt; it
mktemps unique /tmp paths (the skill's fixed-path parallel collision is dead), runs the
pinned codex exec, relays verbatim (never self-answers, reports errors, retries
`--skip-git-repo-check` once), and embeds the check-back contract. Template CLAUDE.md
gpt-5.5 row + base-conventions.md + hooks.md updated; repo-bootstrap 0.37.0.
**Inline fix + fleet re-stamp DONE (2026-07-08, later same day).** codex plugin **0.10.0**
dropped `context: fork` — the skill runs inline, so `Skill(codex)` now works from the main
loop, subagents, and workflow stages alike (regression 6/6 PONG, 0 stubs vs 15/18
baseline); the "never Skill(codex) from subagents" rule is retired. Two lanes:
`Skill(codex)` direct anywhere, `codex:codex-wrapper` for workflow `agentType` routing and
big context gathers. codex@skills re-scoped project→**user** so the plugin loads in every
repo (the second failure leg: fleet Models tables referenced an agent that only existed in
cc-skills sessions). Live enforcement caught up: capt-hook **8.14.0** / general pack
**0.16.0** — nudges + judge rubrics + deliverable_rubric now teach `codex:codex-wrapper`
and fire on the retired sonnet-wrapper shape (8.13.0 was raced by a concurrent session's
tag). repo-bootstrap **0.39.1** carries the template wording: "spawn the
`codex:codex-wrapper` agent … or invoke `Skill(codex)` directly (inline since codex plugin
0.10.0)". Fleet re-stamp of the gpt-5.5 row **DONE across all 31 repos**, gate grep clean
on main@origin (both CLAUDE.md and CLAUDE.src.md; cc-guides-generated repos needed
src+rendered stamped in one commit — see [[cc-guides-generated-claude-md]]). bioqa +
anetaco/backend deliberately excluded (company repos, user's call).
Mechanism details: [[codex-fork-structured-output-leak]].
Related: [[declarative-capt-hooks]], [[fix-framework-not-hooks]], [[dx-over-purity]],
[[shared-working-copy-concurrency]].
